# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration for Automated Backups

# docker-compose.yml - Infrastructure setup
# File: docker-compose.yml
version: '3.8'
services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
    container_name: es-backup-node
    environment:
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - xpack.security.enabled=false
      - path.repo=/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=changeme
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
    volumes:
      - es-data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - es-backups:/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
      - ./config/elasticsearch.yml:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
      - "9300:9300"
    networks:
      - elastic-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1

  minio:
    image: minio/minio:latest
    container_name: minio-s3
    environment:
      - MINIO_ROOT_USER=minioadmin
      - MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD=minioadmin123
    command: server /data --console-address ":9001"
    volumes:
      - minio-data:/data
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
      - "9001:9001"
    networks:
      - elastic-net
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:9000/minio/health/live"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3

  snapshot-manager:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.snapshot-manager
    container_name: snapshot-manager
    volumes:
      - ./config:/app/config
      - ./scripts:/app/scripts
      - ./logs:/app/logs
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_URL=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - S3_ENDPOINT=http://minio:9000
      - S3_ACCESS_KEY=minioadmin
      - S3_SECRET_KEY=minioadmin123
      - S3_BUCKET=es-snapshots
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
      minio:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - elastic-net

volumes:
  es-data:
    driver: local
  es-backups:
    driver: local
  minio-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  elastic-net:
    driver: bridge

---
# File: config/elasticsearch.yml
cluster.name: "backup-cluster"
node.name: "es-backup-node"
network.host: 0.0.0.0

# Snapshot repository paths
path.repo:
  - /usr/share/elasticsearch/backups

# S3 repository plugin settings
s3.client.default.protocol: http
s3.client.default.endpoint: minio:9000
s3.client.default.path_style_access: true

# Cluster settings
cluster.routing.allocation.disk.threshold_enabled: true
cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.low: 85%
cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.high: 90%
cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.flood_stage: 95%

# Recovery settings for snapshot restore
indices.recovery.max_bytes_per_sec: 200mb

---
# File: config/snapshot-config.yml
# Main snapshot configuration file for operations team

# ============================================
# Elasticsearch Connection Settings
# ============================================
elasticsearch:
  hosts:
    - http://elasticsearch:9200
  # Authentication (if security is enabled)
  auth:
    enabled: false
    username: elastic
    password: changeme
  # Connection timeouts
  timeout:
    connect: 30
    read: 120
  # SSL/TLS settings
  ssl:
    enabled: false
    verify_certs: true
    ca_certs: /app/config/certs/ca.crt

# ============================================
# Repository Configurations
# ============================================
repositories:
  # Shared filesystem repository
  filesystem:
    name: fs_backup_repo
    type: fs
    settings:
      location: /usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
      compress: true
      chunk_size: 1gb
      max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
      max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
      readonly: false

  # S3-compatible repository (AWS S3 or MinIO)
  s3_primary:
    name: s3_backup_repo
    type: s3
    settings:
      bucket: es-snapshots
      base_path: elasticsearch/snapshots
      compress: true
      chunk_size: 1gb
      server_side_encryption: false
      buffer_size: 100mb
      max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
      max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
      readonly: false
      # S3 client configuration
      client: default
      # Storage class for cost optimization
      storage_class: standard
      # Canned ACL
      canned_acl: private

  # Secondary S3 repository for cross-region backup
  s3_secondary:
    name: s3_dr_repo
    type: s3
    settings:
      bucket: es-snapshots-dr
      base_path: elasticsearch/dr-snapshots
      compress: true
      chunk_size: 1gb
      client: secondary
      readonly: false

# ============================================
# Snapshot Policies
# ============================================
snapshot_policies:
  # Hourly snapshots for critical indices
  hourly_critical:
    name: hourly-critical-snap
    schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"  # Every hour
    repository: s3_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "production-*"
        - "transactions-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      partial: false
      metadata:
        policy: hourly_critical
        team: operations
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 48h
      min_count: 12
      max_count: 48

  # Daily full snapshots
  daily_full:
    name: daily-full-snap
    schedule: "0 0 2 * * ?"  # Every day at 2 AM
    repository: s3_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      feature_states:
        - "none"
      metadata:
        policy: daily_full
        team: operations
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 30d
      min_count: 7
      max_count: 30

  # Weekly snapshots for long-term retention
  weekly_archive:
    name: weekly-archive-snap
    schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"  # Every Sunday at 3 AM
    repository: s3_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      metadata:
        policy: weekly_archive
        team: operations
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 365d
      min_count: 4
      max_count: 52

  # Filesystem backup (local fast recovery)
  local_backup:
    name: local-fs-snap
    schedule: "0 0 */4 * * ?"  # Every 4 hours
    repository: fs_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "production-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      partial: false
      metadata:
        policy: local_backup
        team: operations
    retention:
      expire_after: 24h
      min_count: 3
      max_count: 6

# ============================================
# Restore Configuration
# ============================================
restore:
  defaults:
    # Wait for completion
    wait_for_completion: true
    # Timeout for restore operations
    timeout: 3600  # seconds

    # Index settings to modify during restore
    index_settings:
      index.number_of_replicas: 0  # Restore with 0 replicas for speed
      index.refresh_interval: -1   # Disable refresh during restore

    # Settings to restore after completion
    post_restore_settings:
      index.number_of_replicas: 1
      index.refresh_interval: 1s

    # Rename pattern for restoring to different index names
    rename:
      enabled: false
      pattern: "(.+)"
      replacement: "restored_$1"

  # Pre-defined restore profiles
  profiles:
    # Full cluster restore
    full_restore:
      description: "Complete cluster restore from snapshot"
      indices: "*"
      include_global_state: true
      ignore_unavailable: false
      partial: false

    # Restore specific indices
    selective_restore:
      description: "Restore specific indices only"
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "production-*"
      include_global_state: false
      ignore_unavailable: true
      partial: true

    # Point-in-time restore
    pit_restore:
      description: "Restore to a specific point in time"
      indices: "*"
      include_global_state: false
      ignore_unavailable: true
      partial: false
      rename:
        enabled: true
        pattern: "(.+)"
        replacement: "pit_$1"

    # Disaster recovery restore
    dr_restore:
      description: "Disaster recovery - restore from secondary repository"
      repository: s3_dr_repo
      indices: "*"
      include_global_state: true
      ignore_unavailable: false
      partial: false

# ============================================
# Monitoring & Alerting
# ============================================
monitoring:
  enabled: true
  check_interval: 300  # seconds

  # Health checks
  health_checks:
    - name: snapshot_age
      description: "Alert if latest snapshot is too old"
      max_age_hours: 6
      severity: critical

    - name: snapshot_size
      description: "Alert on unusual snapshot size changes"
      size_change_threshold_percent: 50
      severity: warning

    - name: repository_health
      description: "Verify repository is accessible"
      check_interval: 600
      severity: critical

    - name: snapshot_failures
      description: "Alert on consecutive snapshot failures"
      max_consecutive_failures: 3
      severity: critical

  # Notification channels
  notifications:
    slack:
      enabled: true
      webhook_url: "${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}"
      channel: "#ops-elasticsearch"
      mention_on_critical: "@oncall-ops"

    email:
      enabled: true
      smtp_host: smtp.company.com
      smtp_port: 587
      from: elasticsearch-backup@company.com
      recipients:
        - ops-team@company.com
        - dba-team@company.com

    pagerduty:
      enabled: false
      service_key: "${PAGERDUTY_SERVICE_KEY}"
      severity_mapping:
        critical: critical
        warning: warning

# ============================================
# Logging Configuration
# ============================================
logging:
  level: INFO
  format: "%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"
  file:
    enabled: true
    path: /app/logs/snapshot-manager.log
    max_size: 100mb
    backup_count: 10
    rotation: daily
  console:
    enabled: true

---
# File: config/s3-credentials.yml
# S3 Client Credentials Configuration
# NOTE: In production, use environment variables or secrets manager

s3_clients:
  default:
    endpoint: "${S3_ENDPOINT:-http://minio:9000}"
    access_key: "${S3_ACCESS_KEY}"
    secret_key: "${S3_SECRET_KEY}"
    region: us-east-1
    path_style_access: true
    protocol: http

  secondary:
    endpoint: "${S3_DR_ENDPOINT:-http://minio:9000}"
    access_key: "${S3_DR_ACCESS_KEY}"
    secret_key: "${S3_DR_SECRET_KEY}"
    region: us-west-2
    path_style_access: true
    protocol: http

---
# File: config/slm-policies.yml
# Snapshot Lifecycle Management (SLM) Policies
# Applied directly to Elasticsearch via API

slm_policies:
  - id: hourly-critical
    body:
      schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"
      name: "<hourly-critical-{now/d}>"
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "critical-*"
          - "production-*"
          - "transactions-*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: false
      retention:
        expire_after: "48h"
        min_count: 12
        max_count: 48

  - id: daily-full
    body:
      schedule: "0 0 2 * * ?"
      name: "<daily-full-{now/d}>"
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
      retention:
        expire_after: "30d"
        min_count: 7
        max_count: 30

  - id: weekly-archive
    body:
      schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"
      name: "<weekly-archive-{now/M}>"
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
      retention:
        expire_after: "365d"
        min_count: 4
        max_count: 52

  - id: local-fast-recovery
    body:
      schedule: "0 0 */4 * * ?"
      name: "<local-snap-{now/d}>"
      repository: fs_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "critical-*"
          - "production-*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: false
      retention:
        expire_after: "24h"
        min_count: 3
        max_count: 6

---
# File: Dockerfile.snapshot-manager
# FROM python:3.11-slim
# WORKDIR /app
# COPY requirements.txt .
# RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
# COPY scripts/ ./scripts/
# COPY config/ ./config/
# RUN mkdir -p /app/logs
# CMD ["python", "/app/scripts/snapshot_manager.py"]

---
# File: scripts/snapshot_manager.py
# This would be referenced by the Docker container
# Content provided as a separate Python script below

---
# File: config/restore-templates.yml
# Pre-configured restore templates for common scenarios

restore_templates:
  # Template: Restore single index
  single_index:
    description: "Restore a single index from the latest snapshot"
    parameters:
      - name: index_name
        required: true
        description: "Name of the index to restore"
      - name: snapshot_name
        required: false
        description: "Specific snapshot name (defaults to latest)"
      - name: repository
        required: false
        default: s3_backup_repo
        description: "Repository to restore from"
    steps:
      - action: close_index
        params:
          index: "{{ index_name }}"
          ignore_unavailable: true
      - action: restore
        params:
          repository: "{{ repository }}"
          snapshot: "{{ snapshot_name | default('latest') }}"
          indices: "{{ index_name }}"
